name: Minutemen
description: Revolutionary discipline — a ball-control offense that never fires until it sees the whites of the end zone.

name: Bridgemen
description: Steel-cable toughness up front; every drive is a span built rivet by rivet.

name: Founders
description: Old documents, new amendments — a franchise that rewrites its playbook every offseason and dares you to keep up.

name: Sentinels
description: A defense that stands post all sixty minutes. Nothing gets through unchallenged.

name: Stockyards
description: Ground and pound with a butcher's patience. Four yards and a cloud of dust, forever.

name: Ironworks
description: Forged in cold weather, quenched in lake wind. December football is their whole identity.

name: Assembly
description: Precision offense off the line — every play a part machined to spec, snapped together at speed.

name: Furnace
description: Blitz-heavy heat. Quarterbacks leave the stadium a few degrees lighter.

name: Wildcatters
description: Boom-or-bust vertical drilling. They strike deep or come up dry — never in between.

name: Current
description: Tempo like a riptide — you don't notice the drive pulling away until you can't touch bottom.

name: Kings
description: Showtime swagger with a soul-deep running game. Saturday-night lights on Sunday afternoons.

name: Stampede
description: Once the run game gets moving, it does not stop for fences.

name: Summit
description: Altitude is the twelfth man. Fourth quarters belong to the team that trains at 5,280.

name: Railmen
description: Everything runs on schedule — screens, sweeps, and a two-minute drill timed to the second.

name: Highrollers
description: Fourth-and-two from their own 40? They've already sent the offense out.

name: Avalanche
description: Quiet, then all at once. Their scoring runs bury games in a single quarter.

name: Firebirds
description: Burn it down, rise again — a pass rush that turns pockets to ash.

name: Aviators
description: First in flight, last to land — the deepest receiving corps in the league.

name: Revelers
description: Trick plays like beads from a balcony. Defending them is a parade you can't police.

name: Armada
description: Broadside football: full volleys of deep shots until something splinters.

name: Blizzard
description: Whiteout conditions, whiteout defense. Visitors lose the field before they lose the game.

name: Riverhogs
description: Low, mean, and impossible to root out of the trenches.

name: Circuit
description: Five hundred laps of no-huddle. The checkered flag is a two-minute drill.

name: Northmen
description: Longships and long drives. They raid, they take, they row home with your win probability.

name: Marshals
description: Law east of the hashmarks. Man coverage with a badge and no backup needed.

name: Cyclones
description: The pre-snap motion never stops spinning. By the time you find the ball it is gone.

name: Scorch
description: Heat that wears you down by the third quarter — conditioning as a weapon.

name: Defenders
description: Remember the red zone. Nobody scores inside their twenty without a siege.

name: Stars
description: Box-office football. Every primetime slot, every highlight reel, every camera.

name: Empire
description: A skyline of a roster — expensive, towering, and lit up at night.

name: Lumberjacks
description: They chop the clock down one carry at a time. Timber is called in the fourth.

name: Squall
description: Sudden weather. A quiet half, then thirty unanswered points blowing sideways.
